overview: The Vignette Filter processes export/import XML documents used with the **Vignette Content Management System**. These XML documents are generated by the `vgnexport` tool. The filter extracts translatable content from named `<attribute>` elements, supporting both bilingual (source+target) and monolingual processing modes.

identification.partsNames.help:
Comma-separated list of values for the `name` attribute of `<attribute>` elements to extract. Each entry identifies a content part in the Vignette XML export.

For example, if the content body is stored in a `MYVCM-BODY` entry, the XML contains:
```xml
<attribute name="MYVCM-BODY">
  <valueCLOB>data</valueCLOB>
</attribute>
```

Add `MYVCM-BODY` to this list to extract that content.
identification.partsNames.values: Comma-separated list of `<attribute>` element name values. Must match the `name` attributes exactly as they appear in the Vignette XML export.
notes: The number of entries here must exactly match the number of entries in **Corresponding filter configurations**. The two lists are positionally paired.

identification.partsConfigurations.help:
Comma-separated list of filter configurations to apply to each extracted part. Each entry corresponds positionally to the matching entry in **Names of the `<attribute>` elements to extract**.

Use `default` for parts that need no sub-filtering, or specify a filter configuration ID (e.g., `okf_html`) for parts containing embedded markup.
identification.partsConfigurations.values: Comma-separated list of filter configuration IDs or `default`. Must contain the same number of entries as the parts names list, in the same order.
examples: `default` — no sub-filtering, extract as plain text
`okf_html` — process content through the HTML filter (for parts containing HTML markup)

dependsOn.condition: must have the same number of comma-separated entries, positionally paired

mode.monolingual.help:
Extracts all `importContentInstance` elements regardless of source/target pairing. When off (bilingual mode), the filter requires both a source and target `importContentInstance` with matching source IDs — entries missing either side are skipped with a warning.

When enabled, the **source ID** and **locale ID** parameters are ignored.

dependsOn.condition: overrides this — sourceId is ignored when monolingual is enabled
overrides this — localeId is ignored when monolingual is enabled

identification.sourceId.help: The value for the `name` attribute of the `<attribute>` element that contains the **source ID** for a content block. This ID is used to pair source and target `importContentInstance` blocks — both must share the same source ID to be extracted together.

identification.localeId.help: The value for the `name` attribute of the `<attribute>` element that contains the **locale ID** for a content block. Used in bilingual mode to identify the language of each `importContentInstance`.

mode.useCDATA.help: Controls whether content in the output is wrapped in CDATA sections. The `okf_vignette-nocdata` configuration disables this for Vignette files that use escaped HTML instead of CDATA sections.

limitations: Very large documents may cause memory problems.
processingNotes: Input and output encodings are **forced to UTF-8** regardless of encoding settings.
In bilingual mode, both a source and target `importContentInstance` element must exist for each block. Entries with only one side are skipped and listed as warnings.
The source and target `importContentInstance` blocks are paired using the source ID defined in the parameters.

examples.title: Vignette XML structure
examples.description: Shows the XML structure of a Vignette export file with named `<attribute>` elements containing translatable content.
